input.prompt:
My MSTest project has a .testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout,
and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.content: `.testsettings` is a legacy format that modern MSTest/vstest ignores. You need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file. Here's the equivalent for your configuration:

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <RunConfiguration>
    <!-- Per-test timeout: 30 000 ms (was TestTimeout in .testsettings) -->
    <TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>
  </RunConfiguration>

  <MSTest>
    <!-- Replaces AssemblyResolution / DeploymentEnabled from .testsettings -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>true</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>

  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <!-- Code-coverage collector (was in AgentRule/DataCollectors) -->
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.SingleFileCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage />
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.reasoningText:
The user has:
1. A `.testsettings` file with test timeout (30s), assembly resolution, and code coverage data collector
2. An MSTest 3.8.0 project targeting .NET 8.0

The `.testsettings` format is legacy and not supported by modern MSTest / vstest. The migration path is to use a `.runsettings` file instead. Let me create the equivalent `.runsettings` file and explain what changed.

Key mappings:
- `TestTimeout` property → `<TestTimeout>` in `<MSTest>` section or `<Timeout>` attribute


- Code coverage data collector → `<DataCollectors>` section in `.runsettings`
- Assembly resolution / deployment → `<DeploymentEnabled>` in `<MSTest>` section (or just remove since modern MSTest handles this differently)

Now I'll create the `.runsettings` file...

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <RunConfiguration>
    <!-- Per-test timeout: 30 000 ms (was TestTimeout in .testsettings) -->
    <TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>
  </RunConfiguration>

  <MSTest>
    <!-- Replaces AssemblyResolution / DeploymentEnabled from .testsettings -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>true</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>

  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <!-- Code-coverage collector (was in AgentRule/DataCollectors) -->
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.SingleFileCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage />
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.content: Now wire the `.runsettings` file into the project so `dotnet test` picks it up automatically:

Here's the mapping from your old `.testsettings` → new `.runsettings`:

| `.testsettings` setting | `.runsettings` equivalent |
|---|---|
| `<Property name="TestTimeout" value="30000" />` | `<TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>` |
| `AssemblyResolution / useLoadContext` | `<DeploymentEnabled>true</DeploymentEnabled>` in `<MSTest>` |
| `CodeCoverage` DataCollector (uri v1.0) | `<DataCollector>` under `<DataCollectionRunSettings>` (uri v2.0) |

**What changed:**
- Created `local.runsettings` with the equivalent settings.
- Updated `TestProject.csproj` to auto-load the `.runsettings` file.
- The old `local.testsettings` can be deleted once you've verified everything works.

Run tests with `dotnet test` — the runsettings will be picked up automatically. You can also pass it explicitly: `dotnet test --settings local.runsettings`.
